own_review.issue: long-to-int truncation: `int len = (int)(count() - transferred())` and `int offset = (int)(position() + transferred())`; `transferred` is also an int field. Regions/offsets > 2GB get truncated (negative/wrapped) offsets and lengths -> wrong data spliced for large files. newSplice accepts long off_in, so the cast is gratuitous.

Returns 0 after fileRegion.open() throws; caller AbstractIoUringChannel.scheduleWrite has `assert numOutstandingWrites > 0` -> AssertionError with -ea (e.g. in CI); without assertions doWrite silently does not set WRITE_SCHEDULED while handleWriteError closes the channel.

ECANCELED regression: ByteBuf path special-cases Native.ERRNO_ECANCELED_NEGATIVE and returns true; file-region path feeds it to ioResult() which throws -> handleWriteError -> channel close on a mere cancellation of an outstanding splice.

JNI_OnLoad now hard-fails (ret=JNI_ERR via done:) if sun/nio/ch/FileChannelImpl or its 'fd' field cannot be resolved -> the entire io_uring native lib fails to load on JDKs where these internals differ, breaking the whole transport, not just FileRegion. Local class refs (fileRegionCls etc.) also never deleted.
Breaking change to public API: public IoUringIoOps constructor gained an int spliceFdIn parameter; external callers of the old 9-arg constructor break.
`result == 0 && fileRegion.count() > 0` uses total count instead of remaining (count() - transferred() > 0); also returning false triggers schedulePollOut which asserts a non-blocking socket.
Leftover commented-out `//@Disabled("Fix me")` above a pointless override that only calls super; copyright header says 2014 (copy-paste).
SQE_FIXED_BUFFER and SQE_PERSONALITY constants added but unused (layout documentation at best).

Only DefaultFileRegion is supported and only when splice is available; custom FileRegion (supported by epoll/kqueue via transferTo) hits UnsupportedOperationException('unsupported message type'). Documented via supportsCustomFileRegion()=false but a runtime limitation worth flagging.
Parameter misnamed 'fileChannel' though it is a DefaultFileRegion.

points.point: scheduleWriteSingle returns 0 on open() failure vs caller assert > 0
points.note: Verified: assert numOutstandingWrites > 0 in AbstractIoUringChannel.scheduleWrite. Matches my finding.

points.point: ECANCELED not handled gracefully for IoUringFileRegion
points.note: Verified real inconsistency vs ByteBuf path.

points.point: Pipe FD leak on error paths (closePipeIfNeeded only in release())
points.note: False: on write error handleWriteError -> close/failFlushed -> ChannelOutboundBuffer safe-releases the IoUringFileRegion, whose release() closes the pipe. No leak path demonstrated.

points.point: Integer truncation for files > 2GB in spliceToPipe
points.note: Verified blocker: silently corrupts offset/length for >2GB regions; fix (use long) is correct since newSplice takes long off_in.

points.point: validateFileRegion not called for splice-to-pipe returning 0
points.note: False: the `result == 0 && count() > 0` check in writeComplete0 runs before handleResult for BOTH phases; a 0-byte splice-to-pipe completion does reach validateFileRegion.
Speculative; the short data is op-local and fully controlled by this code.

missed: JNI_OnLoad hard dependency on sun.nio.ch.FileChannelImpl internals can fail the entire native lib load (whole io_uring transport unavailable)
Breaking change to public IoUringIoOps constructor signature (added spliceFdIn)

points.point: Resource leak in open() if pipe() throws; suggested fileRegion.release() in catch
points.note: False: DefaultFileRegion.open() does not retain (no refcount increment), and the region in the outbound buffer is safe-released on failure/close; the suggested fix would cause a double-release.
Verified: the new test has no @BeforeAll assumeTrue(IoUring.isAvailable()) nor a splice-support check; it will fail rather than skip on unsupported systems. Neither human nor my review caught this.
Kernel cannot splice more out of the pipe than was put in; style-level.

points.point: ECANCELED handling inconsistency for splice vs regular writes
points.note: Core inconsistency verified real; the buffer-cleanup speculation is hedged but the substance stands.

points.point: filterOutboundMessage only accepts DefaultFileRegion, unlike epoll/kqueue transferTo fallback
points.note: Verified; epoll does support generic FileRegion via transferTo.

points.point: JNI reflection on internal JDK classes is fragile
points.note: Real concern (overlaps my JNI_OnLoad finding), though it understates the consequence: a failed lookup aborts the whole native lib load.
transfered() is the long-standing deprecated FileRegion API method, not a typo introduced here; the migration nit is cosmetic.

missed: long-to-int truncation in spliceToPipe for regions > 2GB (the most serious bug in the PR)
scheduleWriteSingle returning 0 on open() failure violating the caller's assert numOutstandingWrites > 0
Breaking change to public IoUringIoOps constructor signature (added spliceFdIn)
exceeds: IoUringFileRegionTest missing assumeTrue availability/splice guard - verified real, absent from both the human review and my own

qualitative: The human reference contains a single condition-ordering nit on filterOutboundMessage that is already satisfied in the diff as shown (the splice-support check comes first), so it was not counted against either candidate. v5 is the stronger review on substance: it caught the two most important correctness bugs (the >2GB long-to-int truncation, graded critical, and the scheduleWriteSingle zero-return assert violation) plus the ECANCELED regression, but it fabricated two absences (a pipe-FD leak that release() via outbound-buffer cleanup actually prevents, and a 'skipped' validateFileRegion check that in fact runs for both splice phases). v6 missed both top bugs entirely and fabricated one absence (the open() leak with a fix that would double-release), but it uniquely found the missing test availability guard - a verified finding that exceeds both the human and my own review - and correctly characterized the epoll/kqueue custom-FileRegion divergence. Net: v5 +1, v6 0; v5 wins on bug-finding depth despite a higher absence-fabrication count.
